commentary: Four activities versus twelve last week — honey, that is a drop, and we are going to acknowledge it before we move on. The swims came in at 1500m each, which is down from the 2000m sessions you were grinding through at Prince Alfred all of last week, so the volume took a hit on both counts. That said, you did stack two solid weight sessions back to back on Thursday and Friday, chest and shoulders into back and legs, which is a clean, intentional training block rather than the beautiful chaos of last week's everything-at-once approach. The real question is whether this is a genuine recovery week or just the beginning of a slide, and I will be watching very closely next week with my arms folded and one eyebrow raised.
metrics_commentary: Okay, three thousand metres in the water with zero runs to show for it — you basically became a merman this week, and honestly, the ocean is lucky. Your HRV is doing something genuinely exciting: sixty-two milliseconds right now against a seven-day average of fifty-one, which means your body is recovering better than it has been all week, and that is your nervous system telling you it liked whatever you did. VO2 Max sitting at fifty-six point seven is strong, legitimately strong — that's the kind of number that makes a certain type of guy at the pool notice you're not just decorative. The only thing I'm raising an eyebrow at is the complete absence of any running or walking, because those legs didn't get painted toes just to dangle off the edge of the lane rope, babe — let's use them.
gym_commentary: Two out of four sessions this week, which is the kind of gap that does not go unnoticed, but here is what does not lie: that bench press PR does not care that it was a short week. Thirty-five point five kilograms on the estimated one-rep max, up from thirty-four point eight, snuck out quietly mid-chaos and that is exactly the kind of stubborn little win that matters. Volume dropped to thirteen thousand seven hundred and five kilograms against last week's twenty thousand one hundred and forty-eight, which is a real dip and worth naming honestly, but with a trap bar deadlift sitting at a hundred and seven point nine and a leg press at a hundred and sixty-eight, the foundation underneath all of this is not going anywhere. Two sessions is not the week Scott drew up for him, but the body that walked into that gym on Thursday is still the body of someone becoming something, and that bench number is proof it is still moving in the right direction.
